title: How does verilator convert internal signals into class attributes ?
body:
I'm using verilator version 4.028 since I'm on wsl and v5 is not available, I would need to compile it from source, I tried in the past and I got many issues with libraries and compiler version.

Since only the documentation for v5 is available it's kinda tough to find any good information. My question is the following :

When you write a system verilog module verilator convert it to c++ and the internal signals are translated into some kind of class attribues. For example let's say I have this :

top module : core
inside it I got a module named exe instanciated a u_exe
Inside exe I got a signal name branch_v From the c++ side it will create : core__DOT__u_exe__DOT__branch_v
The thing is that some signal are not present inside the Vcore.h files, in my case I actually cannot find the branch_v signal. I guess that verilator is doing some optimization but I would like to be able to force it to generate some signal.

I've read about those stuff : /*verilator public*/ , I've done this :

logic                     branch_v /*verilator public*/;

But then all signals dissapear from Vtop.h except the i/o of the module
